empty.title: No tournaments yet
empty.description: Create your first tournament and share the leaderboard with participants.

gettingStarted.title: Getting started with OpenTour
gettingStarted.subtitle: Four steps from an empty dashboard to a live tournament with a real-time leaderboard.

course.title: 1. Choose or create a golf course
course.description: Search for an existing course in the database, or create your own with par and stroke index per hole. Stroke index is required — without it, net scoring can't be calculated.
course.tip: Tip: save your course as ‘public’ so you can reuse it later.

tournament.title: 2. Create your tournament
tournament.description: Give your tournament a name, pick a date and start time, select a course, and choose a scoring format (stroke play, stableford, or matchplay). Add players and flights in the same wizard — manually or via CSV upload.
tournament.tip: Tip: let flights be generated automatically based on the number of players, then adjust manually if needed.

accessCodes.title: 3. Share access codes with recorders
accessCodes.description: Generate an 8-character access code per tournament. Share it via WhatsApp, print, or a QR code on the starting list. Recorders log in with it without an account and can enter scores for all flights.
accessCodes.tip: Tip: a code is valid for 24 hours by default. Lost or misused? Deactivate it and create a new one.

activate.title: 4. Activate the tournament and share the leaderboard
activate.description: Change the status from ‘draft’ to ‘active’ once everything is ready. The leaderboard can be followed instantly via a shareable link — no account or app install needed, on any screen from phone to TV.
activate.tip: Tip: in case of weather or interruptions you can pause the tournament — spectators will see a clear banner with the reason.

gettingStarted.helpDescription: OpenTour is free and open source. Questions, ideas, or bugs can be reported on GitHub.
